generated_oml:
instance OnBoardNumericSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "On-board numeric sensor capturing continuous numeric measurements streamed via MQTT"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MQTTTimeSeriesStream
]

instance SimulinkModelSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Virtual sensing component representing Simulink model outputs"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SimulinkSimulationStream
]

instance MQTTTimeSeriesStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OnBoardNumericSensor
]

instance SimulinkSimulationStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SimulinkModelSensor
]

instance MQTTMessageData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "MQTT message payload containing numeric sensor measurements"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MQTTTimeSeriesStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTIngestionEnabler
]

instance SimulinkSimulationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulink simulation output data payload"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SimulinkSimulationStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkRuntimeEnabler
]

instance MQTTIngestionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler responsible for ingesting MQTT data into the digital twin platform"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataReceptionService
]

instance SimulinkRuntimeEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler that initiates Simulink real-time simulation"
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulationExecutionService
]

instance CodeGenEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler that triggers code generation for C++/Java from SysML and ADL models"
    DTDFVocab:enables CodeGenerationService
]

instance SysMLFunctionalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML functional architecture model describing system requirements and behavior"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CodeGenEnabler
]

instance SysMLLogicalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML logical architecture model describing interfaces and components"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CodeGenEnabler
]

instance MontiArcBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MontiArc behavioral model capturing automata-based component behavior"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CodeGenEnabler
]

instance ACMEArchitecture : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "ACME architecture model representing component and connector structure"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CodeGenEnabler
]

instance SimulinkModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink model representing continuous-time system dynamics"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkRuntimeEnabler
]

instance DataReceptionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that processes received sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:provides DataFreshnessInsight
]

instance SimulationExecutionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that manages Simulink simulation execution"
    DTDFVocab:provides SimulationStateInsight
]

instance CodeGenerationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that generates C++/Java code from models"
    DTDFVocab:provides CodeGenerationInsight
]

instance DataFreshnessInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight on freshness and latency of the received sensor data"
]

instance SimulationStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight on simulation state validity and fidelity"
]

instance CodeGenerationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight on the quality and compatibility of generated C++/Java code"
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The digital shadow is defined as a purely passive, one‑way data consumer; therefore it does not issue command messages back to the physical robot. Consequently there is no virtual to physical control flow, no command format or authentication mechanism, and no feedback or conflict‑resolution protocols are defined."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The overall orchestration of the Digital Twin system follows a Model‑Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) approach that spans multiple abstraction levels—requirements, functional, logical, and product/technical. System architecture is captured through component‑and‑connector models using ADLs such as ACME, AADL, MontiArc or SysML IBDs, which describe the composition and interfaces of system elements. Behaviour is specified either via SysML state machines (v1.7) or MontiArc automata, and is co‑simulated with domain‑specific tools such as MathWorks Simulink or other simulation environments. Integration between the different models is achieved through tool‑specific transformations and co‑simulation setups, allowing each component’s behaviour model, data flow, and control logic to be reconciled within a unified simulation environment."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin lifecycle follows the Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) abstraction hierarchy, progressing through (1) Requirements – where system purpose and constraints are captured; (2) Functional Architecture – represented in SysML activity or block definition diagrams; (3) Logical Architecture – modelled with Architecture Description Languages (ADLs) such as ACME, AADL, MontiArc, capturing component interfaces and connector relationships; and (4) Product/Technical Layer – translated into executable artefacts (e.g., C++/Java code or Simulink models). Transitions between stages are handled by mappings between modelling notations, while governance relies on MBSE standards and model versioning practices."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Sensor data ingestion requires MQTT acting over a TCP/IP network. The architecture presumes an MQTT broker mediating connections between physical sensors and the twin. No further details on bandwidth, topology or redundancy are given."
]
